sections.paragraph:
οἶϰος, ου, ὁ [cp. Lat. vicus ‘a group of houses’]

—1. ‘a structure for habitation’, house, home

—a. Mt 9:6; Mk 7:30; Lk 7:36; Ac 2:2; Ro 16:5; 1 Cor 11:34; Phlm 2; Hb 3:3f; ἐν οἴϰῳ at home Mk 2:1; ϰατ’ οἶϰον from house to house (private residences of the Messianists) Ac 2:46; 5:42, also pl. 8:3; 20:20. Of the temple in Jerusalem Lk 19:46; J 2:16.

—b. w. focus on the view of a house as place of habitation dwelling Mt 12:44; Lk 11:24.

—2. ‘persons inhabiting a house’, house, household, family Lk 10:5; 12:52; Ac 16:31; 1 Cor 1:16; 1 Ti 3:4f; 2 Ti 1:16; Tit 1:11. By extension, of Pharaoh’s realm as household and property Ac 7:10; of ancestral connection Mt 10:6; Lk 1:27; 2:4; Ac 2:36; Hb 8:8. In imagery, of God’s people 3:5; 1 Pt 2:5; the congregation of believers 1 Ti 3:15 (perceived as God’s ‘house church’); 1 Pt 4:17.
